result.codex_reasoning: Codex-sol (MAX reasoning) independently read the changed lines over 3 iterations. Iter1 REVISE: flagged the tighter_retry _call_section leak (multi_section_generator.py:6544) — the reused draft could still regenerate via the kept-fraction retry. Iter2 REVISE: flagged the verifier-driven sentence-repair loop (multi_section_generator.py:6463) which calls repair_sentence -> a fresh OpenRouterClient.generate. Iter3 SOUND: confirmed all four generation LLM paths (distill, primary _call_section, sentence-repair loop, tighter_retry) are now bypassed on reuse; strict_verify still executes unconditionally on the rewritten reused draft (6434); the fresh `multi` flows into 4-role/D8 (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:19236); no verdict is reused (load_a12_checkpoint rejects forbidden verdict keys at 7145/7163); missing/blank/unmatched drafts fall through to fresh generation; py_compile + git diff --check pass. I applied each of codex's exact fixes and re-gated. Converged to CHECKPOINT-FIX-SOUND on iteration 3 (within the 3-loop budget).
result.verify: py_compile: PASS on both files (/usr/bin/python3 -m py_compile scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py -> "FINAL_PY_COMPILE_OK"). AST-parse: PASS on both (ast.parse -> "AST_OK"). git diff --check: CLEAN. Reachability grep confirmed the reuse path is reachable from --resume and that strict_verify runs after injection: (1) sweep gate scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15906 `if _env_flag("PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN", default=False) and resume:` builds _reused_section_raw_drafts from the verdict-free _a12_postgen_payload (loaded at 9551 via load_a12_checkpoint); (2) passed to generator at 16172 reused_section_raw_drafts=_reused_section_raw_drafts; (3) generator param at 9819 -> _run_section(reused_raw_draft=...) at 11026; (4) injection branch `if _reuse_draft_active:` at 6076 sets raw=reused_raw_draft and short-circuits the primary _call_section else-branch at 6364; (5) strict_verify at 6434 runs AFTER the injection (line 6076 < 6434). All 3 added reuse guards present: 6044 (distill), 6463 (sentence-repair loop), 6536/6544 (tighter_retry). Other callers of generate_multi_section_report (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:363, run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py:301) do not pass the new kwarg -> default None -> byte-identical. Pytest is not installed in this worktree (codex noted the same); no test run possible.
result.faithfulness_preserved: The §-1.3 invariant (verification/strict_verify STILL re-runs on the reused drafts; no stored verdict replayed) is preserved by construction and verified at file:line: (a) Only verdict-free DATA is reused — the reuse map is built from _a12_postgen_payload["raw_drafts"], and load_a12_checkpoint (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:7163-7187) RAISES ValueError if any forbidden verdict key (_A12_FORBIDDEN_VERDICT_KEYS at 7149: release_outcome/released/verified/is_verified/final_verdicts/d8_decision/four_role_evaluation) appears at top level, so a verdict can never be loaded. (b) The reused draft is SectionResult.raw_draft, set `raw_draft=raw` at multi_section_generator.py:6860 — the PRE-strict_verify draft — so re-verification runs on the same input a fresh run verifies. (c) strict_verify executes unconditionally on the rewritten reused draft at multi_section_generator.py:6434 (offloaded to a worker thread, identical engine), AFTER the injection point at 6076; the sentence-repair re-verify at ~6579 also re-runs strict_verify. The injection branch sets _draft_directly_tokened=False, so the reused draft flows through the SAME _rewrite_draft_with_spans (6395) + _repair_llm_draft_untokened (6408) + strict_verify (6434) tail the fresh LLM else-branch uses. (d) The fresh MultiSectionResult `multi` is passed into the 4-role/D8 seam at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:19236 (codex-confirmed). NOTHING in the verification path was modified — I only added generation-skip guards. Skipping the sentence-repair loop on reuse is strictly MORE CONSERVATIVE (it only re-adds sentences that re-pass strict_verify; skipping keeps fewer, never more) and mirrors the pre-existing FIX-K `not _evsr` skip of the same loop.
result.diff:
diff --git a/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py b/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py
index 7810e06..f5d1719 100644
--- a/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py
+++ b/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py
@@ -15890,10 +15890,60 @@ async def run_one_query(
         # reusing a draft produced under a different writer/flag config is refused at the source.
         _reuse_postgen_active = False
         _reuse_postgen_reentry = None
+        # ITEM 5 (postgen-resume reuse), DATA-ONLY re-entry via the postgen_checkpoint.json drafts.
+        # The heavy generation_snapshot re-entry (`_load_postgen_reuse_reentry`) is still DEFERRED —
+        # its generator hook (`generate_multi_section_report_from_reused_drafts`) does not exist and
+        # `save_generation_snapshot` is never called in this sweep, so invoking it would FAIL LOUD.
+        # Instead, reuse the ALREADY-WRITTEN, verdict-free postgen_checkpoint drafts (loaded above as
+        # `_a12_postgen_payload`): thread section-title -> raw_draft into the generator so a --resume
+        # SKIPS the per-section draft LLM calls + distill and re-runs the UNCHANGED rewrite +
+        # strict_verify + NLI + 4-role/D8 tail on every reused draft. §-1.3: the checkpoint stores NO
+        # verdict (`load_a12_checkpoint` fails loud if one ever leaked in) and every faithfulness gate
+        # re-runs from scratch on the reused prose — only the generation LLM spend is skipped.
+        # FAIL-OPEN: any error, or a checkpoint whose question drifted, leaves the map empty => full
+        # regeneration. A per-section title with no cached draft also regenerates (per-section fail-open).
+        _reused_section_raw_drafts: "dict[str, str] | None" = None
         if _env_flag("PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN", default=False) and resume:
-            _reuse_postgen_active, _reuse_postgen_reentry = _load_postgen_reuse_reentry(
-                run_dir=run_dir, log=_log,
-            )
+            try:
+                _pg_payload = _a12_postgen_payload  # loaded verdict-free at the resume seam above
+                if isinstance(_pg_payload, dict):
+                    _pg_snap_q = _pg_payload.get("question")
+                    if _pg_snap_q is not None and _gate0_sha_resume(str(_pg_snap_q)) != (
+                        _gate0_sha_resume(q["question"])
+                    ):
+                        _log("[resume]      ITEM 5 postgen reuse SKIPPED (fail-open): checkpoint question drifted...")
+                    else:
+                        _raw = _pg_payload.get("raw_drafts")
+                        if isinstance(_raw, dict):
+                            _reused_section_raw_drafts = {str(_k): str(_v or "") for _k, _v in _raw.items() if isinstance(_v, str) and _v.strip()} or None
+                if _reused_section_raw_drafts:
+                    _log("[resume]      ITEM 5 postgen reuse ACTIVE (DATA-ONLY): ...")
+                else:
+                    _log("[resume]      ITEM 5 postgen reuse: no usable cached drafts (fail-open) ...")
+            except Exception as _pg_reuse_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort; never break the run
+                _reused_section_raw_drafts = None
+                _log(f"[resume]      ITEM 5 postgen reuse SKIPPED (fail-open): {_pg_reuse_exc} ...")
 
         _pathb_gen_tok = _pathb.set_role("generator")
@@ -16114,6 +16164,12 @@ async def run_one_query(
             compose_projection=_compose_proj,
             deliverable_spec=_deliverable_spec,
             scope_spec=_scope_spec,
+            # ITEM 5 (postgen-resume reuse): section-title -> cached RAW DRAFT from the DATA-ONLY
+            # postgen_checkpoint (None unless PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN + --resume + usable drafts).
+            reused_section_raw_drafts=_reused_section_raw_drafts,
             )
         finally:
diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
index 15a4b17..64f8ff5 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
@@ -5903,6 +5903,14 @@ async def _run_section(
     calc_claims: "dict[str, list[str]] | None" = None,
+    # ITEM 5 (postgen-resume reuse): the section's RAW DRAFT reloaded from the DATA-ONLY
+    # postgen_checkpoint.json on a --resume. Non-None+non-empty => SKIP _call_section + distill,
+    # inject cached draft into the UNCHANGED rewrite + strict_verify tail. None => fresh (default).
+    reused_raw_draft: str | None = None,
 ) -> SectionResult:
@@ -6025,8 +6033,15 @@ async def _run_section(
+    _reuse_draft_active = bool(reused_raw_draft is not None and reused_raw_draft.strip())
     distillate = None
-    if _section_distill_enabled() and not _evsr:
+    if _section_distill_enabled() and not _evsr and not _reuse_draft_active:
@@ -6058,7 +6073,28 @@ async def _run_section(
-    if _evsr:
+    if _reuse_draft_active:
+        raw = str(reused_raw_draft)
+        in_tok = out_tok = 0
+        section_atom_catalog = {}
+        _draft_directly_tokened = False
+        logger.info("[multi_section] %s POSTGEN-REUSE: reused cached raw draft ...", section.title, len(raw))
+    elif _evsr:
@@ -6417,7 +6453,14 @@ async def _run_section(
-    if not _evsr:
+    # ITEM 5: SKIP the verifier-driven repair loop on reuse (it calls repair_sentence -> fresh LLM
+    # generate). Faithfulness-SAFE + strictly more conservative (only re-adds strict_verify-passers).
+    if not _evsr and not _reuse_draft_active:
       try:
@@ -6493,6 +6536,12 @@ async def _run_section(
         and not _evsr
+        # ITEM 5: a REUSED draft must NEVER regenerate via tighter_retry _call_section.
+        and not _reuse_draft_active
         and post_filter_fraction < min_kept_fraction
@@ -9774,6 +9823,13 @@ async def generate_multi_section_report(
     storm_outline: list[Any] | None = None,
+    # ITEM 5 (postgen-resume reuse): section-title -> RAW DRAFT map. None/empty => fresh (default).
+    reused_section_raw_drafts: dict[str, str] | None = None,
 ) -> MultiSectionResult:
@@ -10976,6 +11032,14 @@ async def generate_multi_section_report(
                 calc_claims=_outline_calc_claims,
+                reused_raw_draft=((reused_section_raw_drafts or {}).get(plan.title) if reused_section_raw_drafts else None),
             )

NOTE: This is a readable summary of the diff. The FULL verbatim diff is available via `git -C /home/polaris/wt/gateinv diff` and was captured in the session; every hunk above is present with full comment blocks in the working tree.
result.not_covered:
1. CONTRACT SECTIONS are NOT cost-optimized on reuse. The postgen_checkpoint saves drafts for ALL sections, but reuse is threaded only through the LEGACY section runner (_run_legacy_bounded -> _run_section at multi_section_generator.py:11032). Contract sections go through run_contract_section (a separate path) and always REGENERATE + independently re-verify their deterministic/regulatory/narrative streams (contract_section_runner.py:2076/2110/2134). This is INTENTIONAL FAIL-OPEN and codex-confirmed §-1.3-SAFE (contract sections regenerate + re-verify, never replay a verdict) — it only forfeits the cost saving for contract sections. Gate-B force-enables contract generation (run_gate_b.py), so on a Gate-B resume the contract sections still cost their share of generation. Full contract-stream reuse (persist 3 verdict-free streams + a contract reuse branch with per-stream re-verify) is a larger, higher-risk change deferred to a follow-up. This means the ~$2.79 saving is PARTIAL on contract-heavy runs (legacy sections saved; contract sections regenerated).

2. The DEFERRED heavy machinery (generation_snapshot / _load_postgen_reuse_reentry / save_generation_snapshot / the missing generator hook generate_multi_section_report_from_reused_drafts) is left INTACT and INERT. I deliberately did NOT route through it — its generator hook does not exist (getattr returns None -> RuntimeError FAIL LOUD at multi_section_generator.py:8987) and save_generation_snapshot is never called, so enabling it would crash a resume. _reuse_postgen_active now stays permanently False; that branch (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15991 _reuse_postgen_reentry call) is dead but harmless. I chose the lighter, already-written postgen_checkpoint.json as the reuse source instead, which is what the task's precise-state described as WRITTEN-but-only-observed.

3. No live end-to-end resume run was executed (would require a real interrupted paid run + corpus_snapshot on disk + PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN=1). Verification was static: py_compile, AST-parse, grep-reachability, branch-structure trace, and 3 rounds of codex independent file reads. Pytest is not installed in this worktree, so no unit tests ran.

4. Flag-slate identity: on reuse I skip distill (multi_section_generator.py:6044). If a resume runs under a DIFFERENT PG_SECTION_DISTILL setting than the fresh run that wrote the checkpoint, the reduce-marker filter (6352, gated on `distillate is not None`) would behave differently — but since distillate is forced None on reuse, the reduce filter is a no-op and the reused draft goes straight to rewrite+strict_verify. A draft written under distill-ON carries [[finding:]] markers that _rewrite_draft_with_spans still binds; this is the same conservative path. The postgen_checkpoint stores an env_slate but I do NOT hard-assert flag-slate match (unlike the deferred generation_snapshot's assert_generation_flags_match) — a drifted-flag resume would reuse the draft and re-verify it (safe: strict_verify still gates it), only the GATE0 drifted-QUESTION guard is enforced. If strict flag-slate identity is desired, add an assert against _pg_payload["env_slate"]; left out to keep fail-open behavior and because re-verification makes a mismatched-flag draft safe (it just may drop more sentences).
